name: Capital World
terrain: Dense urban — multi-story buildings (garrison), wide avenues (vehicles), elevated overpasses (height advantage), underground tunnels (flanking routes). Destructible: buildings collapse creating rubble cover.
visual: Densely urbanized — city lights cover 40%+ of surface. Orbital rings of stations and defense platforms. Atmosphere hazy with industrial output.
special: Always faction-controlled at campaign start, never spawns neutral. Territory count scales by faction (Necro-Legion: 8 fixed +40% defense bonus; Unity Accord: 18 base + expansion).
narrative: The beating heart of a civilization. Assaulting one is a CAMPAIGN, not a battle — 16–22 territories of increasingly fortified urban terrain where every block is a fortress.

name: Terrestrial
terrain: Mixed — open grasslands, scattered forests (light cover), river crossings (chokepoints), rolling hills (moderate height advantage). The most tactically balanced terrain in the galaxy.
visual: Earth-like — blue oceans, green and brown landmasses, white cloud cover. The galaxy's 'standard' habitable world.
special: 40% random encounter chance. Galaxy's most fought-over real estate — balanced resources, balanced combat.
narrative: Control the majority of Terrestrial worlds and you control the galactic economy. Every faction wants them. Every faction fights to hold them.

name: Volcanic
terrain: Obsidian ridges (hard cover), lava flows (impassable channels), volcanic vents (3% HP AOE every 10 seconds), ash clouds (−10% accuracy beyond 50m).
visual: Angry orange-red surface with active lava flows and volcanic mountain chains. Thick atmosphere of ash and sulfur.
special: 20% chance Rich Veins (double Scrap yield). Highest Scrap income, zero Intel/Influence. Scrap-Horde's preferred operational environment.
narrative: Resource-rich but brutally hostile. Perfect for factions that thrive in harsh conditions. The Horde doesn't just tolerate this environment — they enjoy it.

name: Ocean/Archipelago
terrain: Island warfare — small landmasses connected by bridges and fords. Amphibious assault required for island-to-island movement. Beach chokepoints. Dense tropical interior. Periodic tidal flooding.
visual: 90%+ water surface. Scattered island chains visible from orbit. Planet-spanning storms tracking across the ocean surface.
special: 15% chance Flux Deposit (+3 Intel/cycle). Air superiority is critical — no land-based anti-air covers water gaps between islands. Transports are more strategically valuable here than anywhere else.
narrative: Forces amphibious operations, island-hopping campaigns, and air dominance battles. Factions without transport capacity cannot project power across the water gaps.

name: Jungle
terrain: Multi-layered canopy (30m visibility limit), vine-covered ruins (fortifiable positions), river crossings (natural ambush points), hostile indigenous fauna. All non-infantry units −15% movement speed.
visual: Densely vegetated — unbroken canopy from orbit. Massive river systems threading through the interior. Frequent tropical storms.
special: 50% encounter chance (highest of any type). 25% Indigenous Species event. 20% chance hostile fauna in battles. Infantry-heavy armies excel; vehicle-heavy armies struggle.
narrative: Every territory assault is an expedition into hostile terrain. The environment is as dangerous as the enemy — and the enemy knows how to use it.

name: Desert
terrain: Flat sand plains (minimal cover), rocky mesas (excellent height advantage), deep canyons (natural chokepoints), sandstorms (−20% accuracy during 30-second intervals). Vehicle-friendly flat terrain.
visual: Arid, wind-scoured surface — sand seas, rocky mesas, dry canyon systems. No surface water. Clear atmosphere in clear periods.
special: 15% chance Buried Cache (one-time 10–20⚙ salvage). Ranged dominance — Hammerhead Hover-Tank excels in long sightlines. Melee units struggle crossing open ground.
narrative: Warfare is about MOBILITY and POSITION. Units in the open are exposed; controlling mesas and canyon mouths determines who wins.

name: Ice World
terrain: Cracking frozen lakes (10% vehicle fall-through chance, +15% HP damage and 5-second immobilize), snow drifts (−10% infantry speed), ice caves (underground flanking routes), blizzards (15m visibility for 20 seconds).
visual: Continental ice sheets, frozen oceans, glacial mountain ranges. Thin clear atmosphere between storms. Polar auroras.
special: 20% chance Cryo-Vault (preserved ancient technology). Hostile environment degrades equipment between battles. Necro-Legion immune to cold — significant strategic advantage.
narrative: Tests logistics as much as tactics. The cold is a second enemy that never stops fighting.

name: Ancient Ruins
terrain: Massive megastructures — 10m-thick walls, vehicle-width corridors, underground chamber networks. Partially functional: active energy fields, collapsed sections, dormant defenses that attack BOTH sides.
visual: Remains of a civilization far older than any current faction. Continental-scale geometric patterns visible from orbit. Artificially maintained atmosphere.
special: ALWAYS contain lore fragments. 30% chance Precursor Tech Cache. Highest encounter rate (70%). Critical for the Unification path and Guardian diplomacy. Necro-Legion considers them sacred heritage sites.
narrative: The galaxy's libraries — the primary source of lore fragments required for the Unifier ending. Also the only place Guardian negotiation thresholds can be improved.

name: Gas Giant
terrain: Orbital platform warfare — artificial structures in the upper atmosphere. Enclosed walkways, open bridges. Units knocked off the edge are destroyed (fall into atmosphere). Wind pushes lightweight units toward edges. All cover is constructed.
visual: Massive banded atmosphere, no solid surface. Multiple moons. Planet-spanning storm systems.
special: Cannot be colonized traditionally — orbital platforms and atmospheric mining rigs only. Combat is cramped, dangerous, and claustrophobic.
narrative: Fighting on atmospheric platforms is a nightmare of tight corridors, lethal drops, and constant awareness that the floor is a pressurized lid over a gas giant.

name: Moon
terrain: Low gravity (+20% movement speed, flatter projectile arcs = extended effective range). Craters (natural cover bowls). No atmosphere = no sound (visual cues replace audio warnings). Vacuum damage 0.5% HP/minute without sealed suits.
visual: Small airless body. Cratered surface. Low-gravity dust plains catching the light of the parent planet.
special: Low Gravity environment guaranteed. Strategically valuable for POSITION — a moon orbiting a Gas Giant gives access to atmospheric mining platforms.
narrative: Quick skirmishes — 3–5 territories, fast to conquer, low resource yield. Value is almost entirely strategic location.

name: Orbital Station
terrain: Interior corridor warfare — narrow passageways, sealed bulkheads, cargo bays, reactor rooms. NO vehicles. NO air units. Infantry-only in tight claustrophobic environments where melee contact is nearly guaranteed.
visual: Artificial structure in orbit — space station, drydock, or military installation. Not a natural body.
special: May contain ship repair facilities, communication arrays, or defense weapon platforms. Scrap-Horde +25% melee makes them devastating in this environment. Unity Accord ranged doctrine is nearly useless here.
narrative: The most intense infantry-focused engagements in the game. Every corner is a potential ambush. Every sealed door may have enemies behind it.

name: Dead World
terrain: Flat featureless wasteland — scattered boulders, shallow impact craters, occasional ancient wreckage. Long sightlines, almost no cover. Ranged-dominant.
visual: Barren lifeless surface — no atmosphere, no water, no vegetation. Scarred by ancient impacts or past warfare.
special: 40% chance Derelict Fleet (salvageable or reactivatable ships). 15% hazardous environmental effects. 60% encounter rate. Near galactic core: may contain Guardian ruins.
narrative: The galaxy's graveyards. Something happened here. The remains can be found by those willing to look — and to fight for them.
